violate the law themselves but to heap
disparagement and insult upon the of-
ficials engaged in its administration
and enforcement,”

articles.title: Justice Department Without Authority In Radio Broadcasting
articles.content:
Washington, July 3—(P)—Uider on
opinion rendired by the departinent
of justice today, radio in the United
Siates continues virtually unregulat-
ed.

In substince, it held that the com-
morce depurtment, whieh seught its
sdvice on the subject after congresa
failed to puss apecliic regulatory
legistation, was without authority
under existing lays to control broan-
castIng by the issuance of definite
wave lengths.

This wus similar to the opinion
handed down be the Federal court
at Chicago, in deciding #& (test cane
aeaineat the #Zentth Rarle Carpoara-
ting involving alleged “pirating” of
an unauthorized wave length, While
its full import was not made known,
it Woe Announced at the commerce
department that ita plans would be
made Pubic tomorrow alone with a
xtatement outlining the depariiie"t
future plans.

articles.title: Jap Pleads for Closer Understanding Between Japan and U.S

through Amerten a few months agy.

The prince tock occasion at a din-
ner of the America-Japan Society in
his honor to express for himself and
hig consort, a sister of the Empz2ror,
appreciation of the courtesica shown
them in the United States. He s)'oke
in high terms of hospitallties extend-
ed by President Coolidge and leading
eltizens of New York, Washington,
and other ctties, and urged clomr
atudy in both Japan and the United
Seates of the other nation’s history,
institutions and arts.

articles.title: Ancient Covered Bridge at Madison Destroyed by Flames
articles.content:
(Special to Wennchec Journal)
Madison, July &--The old eovered
byidge over the Rennechec river about

five omiles foun Mndison village
connecting the towns of Aladison
and Ansan, was burned abaut 1 au

M., this morning. The fire originat-
ed from unknown cause at the Madi-
gon end of the bridge.

In 1838 2 corporatino was formed
bid im 1840 the liridge waa erected.
It wus two span, 174 and 169 feet Jong
on granite abutments, and one center
pier, The roof was supported by
three trusts. In the cartier times the
bridge which was a toll bridge was
4 coud tuvestinent for the pramoters
atin, owners, Wut for sume tine ie took
all the receipia to maintain and re-
pur, In avit the State lesistatura

give it free tn Somerset county and
that the couniy mainiain it. A hear-

was held on January 18, that winter.
In March the decision of the hearing
Was announced to the effect that the
county eoukdl pot aceept the $5990
appropriation and maintain the
bride,

Fur some ilme only one side of the
bridge has been open and to dight

much to the farmers in thit section
a8 inany plant corn for the faetory
at North Anson, At the Inst annual

provided that Anson at Its tawn
